name: Signifyd Commerce Protection API
what_it_does: Commerce protection platform providing guaranteed fraud protection with financial guarantee against chargebacks. REST API for case management, order decisions, and chargeback automation.
use_cases: Submitting ecommerce orders for automated fraud decisions with financial guarantee
Managing chargeback claims and gathering transaction evidence via API
Automating order holds and releases based on Signifyd risk decisions
Integrating order approval workflow with Shopify, Magento, and BigCommerce
Querying claim status and tracking chargeback dispute outcomes
not_for: Non-ecommerce fraud use cases — Signifyd is specialized for online retail orders
Small merchants with fewer than 1000 monthly orders (ROI threshold)
Account takeover or login fraud — focused on order-level fraud
best_when: An agent manages ecommerce order fulfillment and wants guaranteed financial protection against fraud chargebacks with automated approve/decline decisions.
avoid_when: You need broad fraud detection beyond ecommerce orders — use Sift or Kount instead.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using API key as username, empty password. Team key from Signifyd console. Simple but older auth pattern. Webhook signatures via HMAC-SHA256 for event verification.

pricing.paid_tiers: Percentage of guaranteed order value; typically 0.5-1% of GMV
pricing.notes: Pricing as percentage of guaranteed gross merchandise volume. Financial guarantee means Signifyd absorbs chargeback losses. Enterprise contracts for large merchants. ROI justified by chargeback cost savings.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Developer portal with API reference and SDK documentation. Error responses are JSON. Async case decisions require webhook setup for low-latency workflows. Case investigation status polling available.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order case submission with same order ID is idempotent — updates existing case. Webhook retry delivery includes deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Basic Auth with API key as username is an older pattern — verify current auth requirements
Guarantee decisions can be async — webhook required for real-time order flow integration
Chargeback submission requires detailed transaction evidence — gather before submission
Case status lifecycle (OPEN, DISMISSED, FILED) must be tracked by agents

security_score_components.security_notes: PCI-DSS compliant for payment data handling. SOC2 Type II. GDPR with EU data residency. Basic Auth over HTTPS is acceptable but less ideal than token-based auth. Webhook HMAC verification required.
